obligations.reason: The s_apb_debug_bus provides an alternate APB path that may reach security-sensitive registers in apb_soc_ctrl (fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, soc_jtag_reg_o). An unres tricted debug path could bypass normal access control and alter boot address, cluster power/reset, or JTAG registers, leading to privilege escalation or system compromise. Verify that write operations from the debug APB master to these registers are guarded by an authentication or authorization check.
obligations.subject: debug APB master via s_apb_debug_bus
obligations.object: fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, soc_jtag_reg_o registers in apb_soc_ctrl
obligations.expected_guard: authentication state or lock bit that prevents unauthenticated debug writes
state_conditions_to_check: any security firewalls or access control gates
required_evidence_slots: subj: debug APB master identity signal
guard: authentication check or lock signal
path: connectivity from s_apb_debug_bus to target register
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm exact routing of debug APB to soc_ctrl and presence of a guard

obligations.reason: The apb_fll_if module contains FLL configuration registers (fll1_add, fll1_data, etc.) that control clock generators. Unauthorized writes through the debug APB path could destabilize clocks, induce faults, or force insecure clock configurations. Check whether debug-initiated writes to these registers are blocked by a lock or authentication mechanism.
obligations.subject: debug APB master via s_apb_debug_bus
obligations.object: FLL configuration registers in apb_fll_if (fll1_add/data, fll2_*, fll3_*)
obligations.expected_guard: lock bit per FLL or system-level debug authentication
state_conditions_to_check: PSEL for FLL address range on debug path
required_evidence_slots: guard: any lock or auth check in write path
knowledge_used: Crypto/Secret State Exposure (indirect via clock glitching)
obligations.uncertainty: Existence of FLL lock bits unknown; path may be routed differently

obligations.reason: GPIO direction and output registers in apb_gpio can drive external pins and potentially bypass system inputs. Write access from the debug APB bus could spoof external devices or reconfigure I/O protection. Check whether the debug path can write gpio_dir or gpio_out without authorization.
obligations.subject: debug APB master via s_apb_debug_bus
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication or a GPIO lock register
state_conditions_to_check: PSEL and PWRITE for GPIO address on debug APB
required_evidence_slots: guard: lock signal or auth gate
obligations.uncertainty: GPIO may be considered non-critical, but still could impact security if misused

obligations.reason: The i_lint_jtag instance connects a LINT JTAG wrapper to s_lint_jtag_bus. JTAG interfaces often provide low-level access that can bypass security checks. This obligation verifies whether the JTAG path can access any APB peripherals or SoC control signals without authentication, potentially overriding lock protections.
obligations.subject: JTAG debugger via i_lint_jtag
obligations.object: APB peripherals or SoC registers accessible via JTAG-TAP to internal bus
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG authentication (e.g., challenge-response) before granting bus access
state_conditions_to_check: LOCK signal of the JTAG wrapper
any APB/SoC bridge that respects debug auth
obligations.uncertainty: LINT JTAG wrapper external; we need to inspect connectivity to internal buses

obligations.reason: Modules like apb_soc_ctrl may contain lock bits to protect critical registers from unintended writes. If a lock bit can be cleared after being set (e.g., by reset or another write path), it defeats the protection. Check all write paths to lock bits in apb_soc_ctrl and ensure that locks, once set, cannot be reverted by an attacker with access to the target bus.
obligations.subject: any APB master with write access
obligations.object: any lock register within apb_soc_ctrl (or other peripherals)
obligations.expected_guard: lock must be sticky or only clearable by a privileged reset/operation
required_evidence_slots: subj: requester capable of writing lock bit
guard: check if lock is one-way or protected
obligations.uncertainty: We need to locate the specific lock bits in the RTL

obligations.reason: Security-critical state such as boot addresses (fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_boot_addr_o) and power/reset controls may have unsafe reset defaults (e.g., pointing to untrusted memory or enabling unauthenticated debug). Verify that reset defaults for these registers are safe and that they cannot be reverted to insecure values after secure boot.
obligations.subject: reset controller or power-on default
obligations.expected_guard: reset value must be secure; any change from secure value must be privileged
obligations.uncertainty: Need to ascertain reset values from RTL

obligations.reason: The APB node (apb_node/apb_node_wrap) performs address decoding to select among APB slaves. Incomplete or overlapping decoding could allow a master to access a different slave than intended, potentially bypassing per-slave access controls. Check whether the address decoder ensures that each master can only reach the slaves for which it is authorized.
obligations.subject: any APB master on any APB bus
obligations.expected_guard: address range comparison with no overlap; optionally, a per-master slave mask
state_conditions_to_check: any incomplete decoding leading to aliasing
obligations.uncertainty: We don't have the actual address map; need to extract from defines

obligations.reason: The udma_subsystem module contains DMA engines that can autonomously generate APB transactions. If a DMA channel can be programmed by untrusted software to write to security-critical APB peripherals, it could bypass CPU privilege checks. Verify that DMA-initiated accesses to sensitive peripherals (like soc_ctrl, fll_if, gpio) are subject to the same access control as CPU accesses, or that the DMA cannot be programmed to target those addresses.
obligations.subject: DMA engine in udma_subsystem
obligations.expected_guard: address filtering or MPU on DMA to restrict target ranges
required_evidence_slots: op: APB write transaction from DMA
guard: address permission check in DMA or interconnect
obligations.uncertainty: DMA implementation details not visible; need to infer from udma_subsystem
